Caption: Literature data on the THR from various LIB tests, categorized into IHG (internal heat generation) calorimetric methods and OCC/CDG. Blue non-filled squared markings indicate that CDG was used in these tests. Data is collected from Refs. [7,8,10,14,15,17,21,29,31–50]. (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)
The open setup used in test series 1. The door was closed during tests and fresh air enters along the opening between the insulation wall and the floor.
The closed and inert pressure vessel setup used in test series 2 and 3.
Oxygen volume concentration measured in 8 different tests in test series 2 by two different analysis techniques.
HRR calculated with three different equations for two different tests on a 55 Ah cell. a) Low CO/CO2 ratio, and b) high CO/CO2 ratio.

HRR calculated for different OCC and CDG equations for a propane reference test and compared with expected HRR based on the propane flow meter. Tabulated E-factors for propane is used in a), and average E-factors in b).
HRR calculated with both OCC and CDG methods for a 55 Ah cell (same as in Fig. 5a).
HRR calculated with both OCC and CDG methods for one of the BEVs in test series 4.
HRR and mass loss data for two different tests on a 157 Ah cell. a) 100 % SOC and slow heating, and b) 75 % SOC and fast heating.
Measured THR, normalized to available electrical energy in the cell, versus ratio of measured total mass of CO and CO2 in the exhaust duct.
